title: Adopt pi-appliance-blueprint: host-bridge, appliance compose, OTA landmines
description: Incorporate docs/design/pi-appliance-blueprint.md: add the root host-bridge (127.0.0.1:9299) for privileged ops, network_mode:host appliance compose so the container reaches it, app relays for update/reboot/streamdeck-restart/health, and rewrite the OTA updater to re-exec itself, sync host-side pieces (deck, helpers, gpio override) separately from the image, be idempotent, and swap atomically. Fixes deck + GPIO not applying on update.
notes: Implemented: host-bridge + unit + installer, appliance compose (network_mode host) selected via COMPOSE_FILE, per-board GPIO override, app bridge client + /system router + streamdeck restart relay, and a blueprint-compliant OTA updater (re-exec, host-side sync, divergence recovery). Kiosk cage/seatd hardening, wlr-randr rotation, touch calibration remain as follow-ons.

title: Phase 2: Automotive test profiles + Stellantis Atlantis High/Mid seed
description: Test profiles carrying year/make/model, VIN(s), the selected CAN interface(s), DBC set, transmit lists, and logic. Seed Stellantis Atlantis High and Atlantis Mid vehicle profiles, focused on infotainment. Set/store VINs per profile.

title: Phase 2: CAN core + Waveshare 2-Ch CAN-FD HAT (python-can/socketcan)
description: The CAN foundation on python-can with the socketcan backend. Bring up the Waveshare 2-Channel CAN-FD HAT (dual MCP2518FD): mcp251xfd overlay, ip link bitrate/dbitrate, can0/can1. A CanBus provider abstraction (open/close/send/recv/set_filters), frame + CAN-FD support, and a bus monitor. Default hardware = Waveshare 2-Ch CAN-FD HAT.

title: Phase 2: PLC-like logic engine
description: A robust logic layer similar to a PLC: rungs/rules with conditions (signal values, inputs, timers, latches, comparisons) driving actions/outputs, evaluated on a scan cycle. Pure, testable rule evaluation; triggers from CAN signals, GPIO, timers, and other actions.

title: Phase 2: Universal action library on the database
description: Generalize actions into a reusable library stored in the DB, not just per-device JSON. An action references a driver (gpio/shell/http/can/lin/logic/macro) plus typed params, is categorized, versioned, and shareable via export. Keep the existing driver registry; move action storage to the DB with import/export.

title: Phase 2: Local database (SQLite) with import/export
description: A locally-hosted SQLite database on the Pi backing the universal action library, CAN signal/message definitions, and automotive test profiles. Import/export the whole database (and per-profile) so setups move between devices. SQLAlchemy models, a migrations-safe init (never reset a live install), and REST + UI for import/export.

title: Phase 2: Full test & automation platform (CAN / automotive)
description: Turn AutoPi into a full test and automation platform. Universal action keys built on a library backed by a local database (SQLite on the Pi, import/export). PLC-like logic engine. Robust CAN bus support: aggregate all available automotive CAN libraries with the ability to customize them and add custom commands; support multiple CAN architectures and test-equipment (default Waveshare 2-Ch CAN-FD HAT, plus other CAN hats, LIN, Ethernet/DoIP, and external hardware like PEAK PCAN and Vector). Signal simulation (periodic and one-shot), CAN firewall/modify (inhale/exhale / man-in-the-middle). Automotive test profiles with year/make/model and VINs; start with Stellantis Atlantis High and Atlantis Mid, focus on infotainment.

title: Visual Stream Deck / Start menu layout editor with scaling
description: Build the deck-shaped, paginated key editor with a categorized palette, model/rotation/brightness controls, and a connected-deck indicator. Grid scales to the selected model (6/15/32) or the connected deck, and reshapes on rotation. Shared between the Start Page and Stream Deck surfaces.
close_reason: Built the deck-shaped, paginated editor with categorized palette, model/rotation/brightness controls, connected-deck scaling via controller status reports, and shared Start Page / Stream Deck surfaces.

title: Port PantryRaider UI shell and menu structure to AutoPi
description: Match the PantryRaider interface: vendor the Bootstrap dark theme + icons, port the base chrome and floating nav, rebuild the start menu to match start.html, seed a handful of test keys for the start menu and Stream Deck, ensure Stream Deck services install, and port the OTA updater.
close_reason: Ported the Bootstrap dark shell (blue accent), sectioned settings menu with food sections removed, seeded demo keys on both surfaces, wired driver list, and added the autopi-update OTA script. Per-driver action forms (AutoPi-0jw) and deck face parity (AutoPi-3jm) remain open.

title: Phase 2: CAN/automotive UI (bus monitor, sim, profiles)
description: The web UI for the automotive side: a live bus monitor, the transmit/simulation panel, the firewall rule editor, DBC management, and profile selection (year/make/model/VIN). Matches the existing Bootstrap dark shell.

title: Phase 2: Automotive CAN library aggregation + custom DBC/commands
description: Aggregate available automotive CAN libraries (opendbc and other open DBC/ARXML sources), import DBC/database files, edit and extend them, and add custom commands/PIDs. A parser layer (cantools) for encode/decode; user-editable definitions stored in the DB.

title: Phase 2: CAN signal simulation (periodic + one-shot)
description: Simulate CAN signals: periodic transmit tasks (cyclic messages at a period) and one-shot sends, encoded from signal values via DBC. Start/stop/edit periodic sims; a transmit list per profile.

title: Phase 2: CAN firewall / modify (inhale-exhale, man-in-the-middle)
description: Bridge two CAN channels and firewall/modify traffic in flight: allow/block/rewrite messages by id or signal, inject or drop, and perform inhale/exhale (capture a bus into the tool, replay/modify, exhale to the other side). Rules built on the DB and logic engine.

title: Phase 2: Multi-hardware CAN/LIN/Ethernet + external interfaces
description: Extend the provider abstraction to multiple CAN architectures and test equipment: other CAN hats, PEAK PCAN (python-can pcan), Vector (python-can vector), LIN, and automotive Ethernet/DoIP. A registry so a profile selects its interface; graceful absence when hardware/driver is missing.

title: Phase 1: validate the on-device installer end to end on real Pi hardware
description: install.sh + provisioners are written to proven patterns but not yet run on a real Pi. Flash Raspberry Pi OS Lite, run the one-liner, and verify Docker install, stack up, Wi-Fi AP fallback, Stream Deck controller (udev+systemd), and the kiosk display all come up.

title: Phase 1: Stream Deck key-face rendering parity
description: render.py is a minimal solid-color tile with a label. Port the richer key faces from the source project (gradients, glyph/icon rendering, live faces) on top of the controller loop without changing the poll/dispatch structure.

title: Phase 1: per-driver setup forms in the layout editor
description: The action editor currently posts raw driver params. Build per-driver forms from each driver's param_schema (gpio pin/mode, shell command/timeout, http method/url/headers/body, macro member picker) so actions can be created and edited in the UI without hand-writing JSON.

title: Phase 2: Diagnostics (ISO-TP / UDS / OBD-II)
description: Diagnostic transport and services on the CAN core: ISO-TP framing, UDS (diagnostic sessions, read/write DID, routine control) and OBD-II PID reads, bound to actions and profiles.

title: Phase 2: CAN driver via python-can + Waveshare 2-Ch CAN-FD HAT
description: Implement drivers/can.py on python-can with the socketcan backend: bring up can0/can1 (mcp251xfd overlay, ip link bitrate), send frames, ISO-TP/UDS requests, and a bus monitor. Gated on Phase 1 landing on hardware. See docs/ROADMAP.md.

title: Phase 1: kiosk hardening (rotation, idle blanking, on-screen keyboard)
description: setup-kiosk.sh installs a minimal cage/Chromium kiosk. Add display rotation, idle blanking/wake, and an on-screen keyboard for touch, mirroring the source appliance behavior.
